behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all key file paths (src/Controls/Maps/src/Controls.Maps.csproj, src/Controls/Maps/README.md), required literal strings (Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Maps, Maps and mapping support, UseMauiMaps, dotnet add package, etc.), platform directories (net, net-android, etc.), and schema keys (PackageReadmeFile, PackageId, IsPackable, PackageTags, Description). The tests check these same paths, strings, and structural elements, all discoverable from instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly tells the agent to add PackageReadmeFile property, add a <None> item with Pack='true', and specifies exact content requirements like 'must include dotnet add package Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Maps' and 'must show UseMauiMaps()'. It prescribes the exact fix rather than just describing the problem. The csproj Metadata Requirements section literally says 'declare a PackageReadmeFile property pointing to README.md and include README.md as a <None> item with Pack="true"'.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for structural properties (XML validity, presence of PackageReadmeFile, README content containing required keywords) rather than exact line positions or specific variable names. Alternative README implementations with different wording/structure would pass as long as they contain the required keywords and meet length requirements. The csproj tests check for property presence, not exact XML formatting.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The tests are almost entirely text-matching: checking file contents with string 'in' operators (e.g., test_readme_content_installation checks 'dotnet add package Microsoft.Maui.Controls.Maps' in content), XML parsing of csproj structure, and file existence checks. No code is actually executed, no builds are run, no imports happen. The git subprocess tests only verify repo state, not code behavior. This is fundamentally a text/file-content checking task.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests require creating a new README.md file (which doesn't exist at the base commit) and modifying the csproj to add PackageReadmeFile. A stub/empty file would fail length checks (>=500 chars), keyword checks, and structural requirements. The csproj modifications require adding new XML elements that don't exist in the base commit.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The tests cover all behaviors described in instruction.md: README existence, length (>=500 chars), installation instructions, UseMauiMaps mention, feature coverage (pin/polygon/polyline/circle/MapClicked/MapType/IsShowingUser), code examples (XAML maps:Map), documentation link, markdown structure, csproj metadata preservation, PackageReadmeFile declaration, and None item with Pack=true.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Repo CI Gates, fail-to-pass sections), descriptive function names (test_readme_exists, test_readme_content_installation, test_csproj_package_readme_declared), and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task produces markdown documentation and XML project file modifications, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB output.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, commands, variable names, and property names in the instruction are correct and consistent with the test expectations.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names the exact XML elements to add: 'declare a PackageReadmeFile property pointing to README.md and include README.md as a <None> item with Pack="true"'. This goes beyond describing what's broken and specifies the exact implementation details of the fix.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold diff is localized: adding a PackageReadmeFile property to an existing PropertyGroup and adding a None item in an ItemGroup in the csproj, plus creating a new README file. It doesn't rewrite the full csproj or contradict other files.
